items.briefing:
Australians are being warned about a rise in scams impersonating the Australian Taxation Office (ATO) and myGov. These scams typically involve fake messages or calls asking you to verify personal details or make urgent payments. Scamwatch is actively tracking these and urging people to stay alert, particularly as tax time approaches. Separately, the Australian Communications and Media Authority (ACMA) has announced its 2026–27 compliance priorities, with a focus on better protecting consumers from harmful online activity.

If you receive any unexpected contact claiming to be from the ATO or myGov, do not click links or provide personal details — go directly to the official website to verify.

featured.title: Australian Taxation Office (ATO) and ‘myGov’ impersonation scams - Scamwatch
featured.quote: ATO and myGov impersonation scams pose direct financial risks to millions of Australian taxpayers.
